votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the two disjuncts of the Coq specification, checking for a sorted array when res == -1, and checking for a specific violation index when 0 < res < len(arr).

The Python code correctly implements both cases of the Coq specification: the case where res is -1 (the entire list is non-decreasing) and the case where res is a positive index (the element at res is less than the previous element, and the suffix starting at res is non-decreasing). The index bounds and comparisons match the Coq spec exactly.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification, checking the same conditions for res == -1 and 0 < res < len(arr).

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec. When res=-1, it checks all consecutive pairs are non-decreasing (matching the Coq condition with >= which means 'not less than'). When 0<res<len(arr), it checks arr[res]<arr[res-1] and that all subsequent pairs are non-decreasing. The Python returns False for invalid res values, which correctly indicates the spec is not satisfied. The logic matches the Coq disjunction of the two cases.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec: it returns True when res=-1 and the array is non-decreasing from index 1 onward, or when 0<res<len(arr), arr[res]<arr[res-1], and all subsequent pairs are non-decreasing. The Coq uses >= for non-decreasing checks (nth i >= nth (i-1)), which means arr[i] >= arr[i-1], so the violation condition is arr[i] < arr[i-1], matching the Python. The Python returns False for any other case, correctly rejecting invalid res values.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec. When res=-1, it checks all consecutive pairs are non-decreasing (matching the Coq condition with >= which means 'not less than'). When 0<res<len(arr), it checks arr[res]<arr[res-1] and that all subsequent pairs are non-decreasing. The Python returns True/False matching whether the Coq Prop would hold for those concrete inputs.
Matches Coq: res = -1 iff array is nondecreasing; otherwise 0 < res < len(arr) with a drop at res and nondecreasing thereafter; else False.

The Python code implements the same two cases: (1) res = -1 and the array is nondecreasing, and (2) 0 < res < len(arr) with a strict drop at res and nondecreasing thereafter. Index bounds and comparisons align with the Coq spec for all concrete inputs.

Matches the two Coq disjuncts: res = -1 enforces non-decreasing across all adjacent pairs; otherwise 0 < res < len(arr) requires a strict drop at res and non-decreasing thereafter. Indices and comparisons align with Coq’s ranges and semantics.
